summary_one_line: Input needs a valid GDEF table whose ClassDef format field equals 2 to select case 2 of the switch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid GDEF table (FOURCC 'GDEF' at offset 0x0c) whose ClassDef subtable carries format==2, so OT::ClassDef::get_class dispatches into case 2 (format2.get_class) rather than the default return-0 arm.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the same parsed sfnt header and carries the literal bytes 47 44 45 46 ('GDEF') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, then format/structure bytes that drive 540 entries through case 2; the format2 get_class implementations are hit 540 times by W and 0 by L.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short, unstructured byte runs (e.g. a 6-byte seed and 13-byte seed) that never place the 'GDEF' tag at offset 0x0c; they take the default arm 1020 times (L T=1020 on default, T=0 on case 2), so format2 is never selected.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer from the format-tag and format-field integer compares and substitutes the matching constants (e.g. the 'GDEF' tag and the format==2 discriminant) into the input, building the structured header that naive/value_profile cannot synthesize by blind havoc; value_profile lacks the I2S substitution mutator entirely and cannot place the exact multi-byte tag by feedback alone.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is case 2 of a switch on u.format inside OT::ClassDef::get_class, and the default arm is the loser-taken side.
The switch discriminates on u.format and the loser inputs fall through to the default return-0 arm.
Winner inputs drive case 2 540 times while losers take the default arm 1020 times and never reach case 2.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1997  T=540 F=0  T=0 F=1020  case 2: return u.format2.get_class (glyph_id);  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'GDEF' table tag at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.
At offsets 0x0c-0x0f every winner seed holds the GDEF FOURCC while losers hold scattered unstructured bytes.
evidence_trail.cited_section: BYTE DIFF (W vs L at common offsets)

evidence_trail.claim: The format2 get_class implementations are invoked 540 times by the winner set and 0 by the loser set.
Loser-blocking seeds are short unstructured byte runs lacking the parsed GDEF header.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=003817559785f774, size=6 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=0s, mutation_op=ByteDecMutator,WordAddMutator):

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one CMP operand at offsets matching the other, the technique distinguishing the winner from naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, so the I2S-vs-value_profile delta is the substitution stage.
Both decisive pairs differ from their loser by exactly the I2S technique.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5481 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 1c44206ca833678e`; the 27-level ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 25 and depth 26, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose case-2 dispatch depends on a single-byte format==2 discriminant (no multi-byte tag gate) where naive resolves the branch as fast as cmplog/vpc, showing the I2S substitution is not what places the format discriminant.

weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly observe which exact CMP operands (the GDEF tag vs the format==2 byte) I2SRandReplace substituted, since the dash-row floor only confirms an unwrapped I2S find existed in the chain, not its target offset.
